description_long: This irregularly shaped stone features a rugged, layered surface with prominent ridges and grooves, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. The coloration is predominantly off-white to light gray, with some darker streaks and patches visible on one side, suggesting mineral deposits or surface staining. The stone is relatively flat and wide, with a thin profile when viewed from the side, resembling a large, flaked slab or a piece of sedimentary rock. Its surface texture appears rough and uneven, with visible striations and undulating contours that catch the light differently across its faces. The edges are jagged and asymmetrical, lacking any signs of artificial shaping or smoothing. This stone would likely be found as a standalone feature or as part of a natural or landscaped urban setting, such as a park or garden.
description: A large, flat, off-white stone with rugged, layered ridges and rough, jagged edges. One side features darker mineral streaks, enhancing its natural, weathered look.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and flat with visible layered ridges and a mostly off-white surface, showing its full face and natural contours.
description_view_1: From the side, the stone looks thin and elongated, highlighting its slab-like profile and the pronounced layering along its edge.
description_view_2: This view reveals a darker, streaked surface with more pronounced mineral deposits, emphasizing the stone's weathered and variegated appearance.
description_view_3: Another side profile, similar to view 1, showing the stone's thinness and rough, uneven edge with subtle surface texturing.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural layering and veining, with some darker mineral streaks and mottling on one side.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible surface erosion and mineral staining indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped areas.
